commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=12a963236df62a380a3f7b7e2d09a4497680cf66)
commands.review_summary:
The PR filters `/status` configured fallback display entries that exactly match the selected model and adds unit tests for that filter.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +52. Total +56 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: `buildStatusMessage()` displays configured fallbacks independently of user override strictness, while runtime policy returns no fallbacks for user-pinned model selections. I did not run a live `/status` flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to adjust the behavior and add real after-fix proof; automation should not repair this while the external-PR proof gate is contributor-owned.

target.title: fix(status): exclude session-selected model from fallback display list
target.body:
Fix #88039.

When a non-default model is selected via `/model` for a session override, the
`/status` display still includes that model in the `🔄 Fallbacks:` line. This
creates a self-referential fallback chain where the first fallback candidate
is the already-active model, wasting an API call and delaying recovery.

**Root cause:** `buildStatusMessage()` in `status-message.ts` reads the
configured fallback array from `args.agent.model.fallbacks` and displays it
verbatim without filtering out the session-selected model.

**Fix:** Filter the configured fallbacks array to exclude any entry that
matches the session-selected model (case-insensitive via the existing
`normalizeLowercaseStringOrEmpty` helper). When all fallbacks match the
selected model, the fallbacks line is hidden entirely.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Session-selected model appears in its own fallback list display, creating a self-referential fallback chain.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-88039`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-88039
  CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  grep -n "filteredFallbacks" dist/*.js
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/status/status-message.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The fix is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "filteredFallbacks" dist/status-message-C3KbOn4t.js
  458:const filteredFallbacks = (() => {
  462:const configuredFallbacksLine = filteredFallbacks?.length ? `🔄 Fallbacks: ${filteredFallbacks.join(", ")}` : null;
  ```

  Terminal confirms the filter works correctly:

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/status/status-message.test.ts
  ✓ |unit-fast| src/status/status-message.test.ts (5 tests) 185ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  5 passed (5)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Terminal output confirms the fallback filter is compiled into the production bundle and all five tests pass (two existing `formatFastModeLabel` tests plus three new fallback-filtering scenarios): filtering the selected model, showing all fallbacks when no match, and hiding the line when all match.
- **What was not tested:** Live gateway with real Telegram channel and model failover (requires provider credentials and channel setup).
